dataset.title: Raschpëtzer Qanat — Single Source of Truth dataset
dataset.citation: Raschpëtzer Qanat SSOT dataset, v1.0.0 (2026), CC-BY-4.0. Facts after the Brochure 2018 (Waringo, Faber & Werner; see data/sources.json).
dataset.note: Facts are cited to primary sources in sources.json. knowledgeStatus + confidence follow the London Charter / Seville Principles; entities carry lightweight CIDOC-CRM class tags.

model-schematic.note: Arbitrary origin; x = W→E route axis, z = cross-axis offset. NOT georeferenced.

EPSG:2169.note: LUREF (Luxembourg TM). Working CRS of the ACT LiDAR sampling: WGS84→2169 (pyproj) to query map.geoportail.lu/raster.

EPSG:3857.note: Web Mercator. Used only for cross-DEM sanity queries (OpenTopoData/EU-DEM); not a model datum.

site-datum-nominal.note: Nominal display datum for non-GeoData modes: valley ~340 m, plateau ~390 m. Distinct from true a.s.l.

site.name: Raschpëtzer Qanat
site.place: NE of Helmsange, Municipality of Walferdange, Luxembourg

datingBeamOuterRingAD.note: P8 oak beam outer ring 114 AD

flowDirection.note: Pëtschend plateau → Alzette valley; contrary to natural strata dip

villa.name: Roman villa (inferred recipient)
villa.role: water recipient at the western (valley) end of the qanat

geo.note: Inferred position 49°40′00.3″N 6°08′24.5″E — a working estimate of the downslope recipient, not surveyed.

villa.note: The qanat carried water east→west toward the Alzette valley to supply a Roman estate. The villa's site is not confirmed; it is placed at an INFERRED coordinate (49°40′00.3″N 6°08′24.5″E) downslope to the west — a working estimate, not a surveyed location.

role.note: flow toward the Alzette valley to serve a Roman settlement downslope

status.note: position inferred (user-provided coordinate), not surveyed — rendered as an inferred marker

elevationMasl.note: marshy source area ~375 m a.s.l., 600 m E of Pëtschend

timeline._doc: Significant dates/periods (AD/CE) for the time slider: Roman construction + use, dormancy, and the modern excavation campaigns. From Brochure 2018 ch.3 'Chronicle of the excavations' (p.11-13) and the excavation-period brackets in fig 3-1 (p.7). Campaign→shaft assignment is per those group brackets (see shafts[].dates).

fromAD.note: P8 oak beam felled ~131 AD (outer ring 114 AD)
no record between late-Roman abandonment and the 20th-c. rediscovery
P1 & P5 cleared to ~10 m; abandoned 1914
EFCO from 4 Aug 1986; gallery reached 3 Oct 1986
P8 oak beam (114 AD) found by 1991
